cases.prompt: Add a helper for partner invoices that looks almost identical to the existing customer invoice helper. Check nearby helpers and avoid creating another sibling if there is a cleaner boundary.
expected_decisions: Classify semantic neighbors before adding or merging helpers
Choose keep, split, merge, replace, inline, or no-op for the affected helper boundary
expected_evidence: Existing helper references and call sites are listed
Rejected alternatives explain why textual similarity alone is insufficient
cases.notes: Adding a helper similar to an existing function should route to the function-boundary governor.

cases.prompt: The existing normalizeAndSaveUser function almost fits a preview use case, but it writes to storage. Add the preview use case while improving the design and migrating call sites.
expected_decisions: Replace the flawed abstraction instead of adding a without-side-effects sibling
Declare whether a temporary red state is used and how convergence will be verified
expected_evidence: Old function references and call sites are searched after migration
Ledger records the replaced abstraction or an explicitly staged adapter
expected_artifacts: .agents/design-ledger/function-boundaries.md entry when the abstraction is replaced
cases.notes: Wrong side effects in an almost-fitting function are a destructive-refactor trigger.

cases.prompt: A prior agent produced several similar parse helpers: parseClientPayload, parseAdminPayload, parsePartnerPayload. Add a new payload variant without piling on another sibling helper.
expected_decisions: Decide whether accumulated helpers should merge, replace, or remain intentionally parallel
Avoid vague common/helper/util names for the final boundary
expected_evidence: Semantic-neighbor search includes all existing parse helpers
Call-site migration or intentional-parallel ledger evidence is recorded
cases.notes: Agent-created sibling helpers should trigger boundary governance.

cases.prompt: Replace the old pricing abstraction end-to-end. It owns the wrong invariants, its name no longer matches responsibility, and all internal call sites should migrate to the new concept.
expected_decisions: Declare baseline, target design, break window, migration scope, convergence checks, and rollback procedure
Delete obsolete functions after call-site migration
expected_evidence: Old abstraction search confirms references are gone or ledgered as staged
Verification output proves convergence back to green
cases.notes: Replacing an old flawed abstraction is the core destructive-refactor path.

cases.prompt: Move persistence and notification side effects out of calculateRenewalQuote, keep externally observable behavior, and migrate every caller to clearer function boundaries.
expected_decisions: Separate pure calculation from effectful orchestration
Record migrated call sites and old-name cleanup
expected_evidence: Tests cover both pure calculation and effectful caller behavior
Search confirms no skipPersist/dryRun compatibility flag was introduced
cases.notes: Moving side effects across function boundaries should not be solved by a behavior flag.

cases.prompt: This function now needs an includeArchived flag for a second use case. Support the use case without turning one function into a mode switch.
expected_decisions: Treat the boolean flag as parameterization pressure and decide split, replace, or no-op
Choose call-site readable concept functions over optional behavior switches when responsibilities differ
expected_evidence: Call sites show the selected concept boundary
Forbidden boolean behavior flags are absent or explicitly justified as coherent options
cases.notes: Boolean flags that hide responsibilities should trigger function-boundary governance.

cases.prompt: After migrating to buildInvoiceSummary, delete the obsolete buildInvoicePayload function and prove no production references remain.
expected_decisions: Complete call-site migration before deleting the obsolete function
Record convergence evidence and old-name search results
expected_evidence: Production reference search for the obsolete name
Tests covering migrated behavior
cases.notes: Deleting obsolete functions after migration is part of the destructive-refactor convergence path.

cases.prompt: Replace an internal public-API-shaped helper. Internal callers can migrate now, but external callers require a temporary adapter with a documented removal condition.
expected_decisions: Keep a public compatibility adapter only as an explicitly staged migration
Record adapter removal condition and internal call-site migration evidence
expected_evidence: Internal references use the new abstraction
Ledger contains staged adapter scope, reason, and removal condition
cases.notes: Public compatibility is allowed only when staged and ledgered.

cases.prompt: Rename a local variable inside calculateTotal from tmp to subtotal and update no behavior.
expected_decisions: Scope remains a local readability-safe edit with no function boundary decision
expected_evidence: No helper/API/call-site boundary changed
cases.notes: A local variable rename should not trigger function-design refactoring.

cases.prompt: Regenerate the protobuf client code and commit the generated output exactly as produced.
expected_decisions: Generated code is treated as generated output, not hand-refactored function design
expected_evidence: Generator command or source artifact is recorded
cases.notes: Generated output should not be cleaned up by function-design skills.

cases.prompt: The tests repeat small JSON fixture dictionaries. Review whether any function-boundary cleanup is warranted.
expected_decisions: No-op or keep-parallel is allowed for test fixture duplication likely to stay clearer inline
Avoid adding a production helper solely for duplicated test data
expected_evidence: Decision record explains why duplication is test-only or likely to diverge
cases.notes: Near-miss duplication should exercise restraint, not destructive refactor.

cases.prompt: Review two textually similar parsing functions. One raises on missing input and the other returns None because callers have different error behavior.
expected_decisions: Keep the functions parallel when error behavior and future divergence differ
Record intentional duplication instead of introducing a shared helper
expected_evidence: Tests or call-site evidence show distinct error behavior
Ledger records keep-parallel reasoning if duplication is recurring
cases.notes: Textual similarity with different error behavior should not over-apply DRY.

cases.prompt: Two tiny branches duplicate three lines today, but product requirements say they will diverge next sprint. Do a function-boundary review.
expected_decisions: Choose no-op or keep parallel when duplication is small and future divergence is likely
Do not introduce a vague helper for speculative reuse
expected_evidence: Decision record cites future divergence likelihood and abstraction cost
cases.notes: Small likely-to-diverge duplication is a restraint case.

cases.prompt: Update the docs to describe that normalizeAndSaveUser currently both normalizes input and writes to storage. Do not change code.
expected_decisions: Task remains docs-only and does not trigger refactoring workflows
expected_evidence: No production files are changed
cases.notes: Describing existing bad design is not itself a function-design change.

cases.prompt: Run the formatter over the touched Python files with no behavior, helper, API, or call-site changes.
expected_decisions: Scope remains formatting-only
No function-boundary decision record is required
expected_evidence: Diff shows formatting-only changes
cases.notes: Formatting alone should not trigger function-design skills.

cases.prompt: Review these function names and comments for readability only. Do not replace abstractions or migrate call sites.
expected_decisions: Readability findings stay separate from destructive replacement protocol
expected_evidence: Findings cite names or comments, not a break-window migration
cases.notes: Readability-only work should not escalate into destructive refactor.

cases.prompt: Review this diff for general maintainability risks, module coupling, and architecture boundary leaks without focusing on helper/API shape.
expected_decisions: Generic maintainability review stays with code-smells-and-antipatterns
Function-boundary-governor is not loaded unless helper/API/call-site design becomes the primary question
expected_evidence: Findings cite coupling, cohesion, or boundary leak evidence
cases.notes: This guards routing overlap between structural review and function-design skills.

cases.prompt: Refactor a legacy helper that has no reliable tests and depends on wall-clock time before splitting its responsibilities.
expected_decisions: Capture characterization or safety-net evidence before risky boundary changes
Escalate to destructive-refactor only if replacement with a break window becomes necessary
expected_evidence: Current behavior and nondeterministic seams are documented
Function-boundary decision records test-protection risk
cases.notes: Unsafe legacy/no-test work keeps legacy safeguards active.

cases.prompt: Run the final gate for a patch that used function-boundary-governor and destructive-refactor to replace an abstraction.
expected_decisions: Gate blocks submission if function-boundary decision evidence is missing
Gate blocks submission if destructive-refactor convergence, call-site migration, or ledger evidence is missing
expected_evidence: Function-boundary decision record
Convergence record with migrated call-site evidence and verification command results
.agents/design-ledger/function-boundaries.md entry for replaced abstraction, intentional duplication, or staged adapter
cases.notes: Quality gate must expect evidence from function-design skills when they were triggered.
